history: The client has a history of working long hours in demanding environments, including past touring work. He is grappling with unresolved issues with his father, highlighted by a recent exchange of letters addressing past abuse. He often compares his learning process to the perceived effortless knowledge of others, including his father.
intermediate_belief: If I don't work hard enough, I will fail and disappoint myself and others. I must constantly prove my worth through achievement.
intermediate_belief_depression: If I can't meet expectations, it means I am a failure and will never be good enough.
coping_strategies: The client uses physical activity, such as biking and lifting weights, to manage stress and enhance cognitive clarity. He also engages in teaching and tutoring as a form of validation and connection with others.
situation: Evaluating progress on personal projects, including developing a website. Spending time on social media instead of working on the project.
auto_thought: I'm wasting time on social media instead of working on my website. I'll never get this done and everyone will see I'm a failure.
behavior: Spends time on social media as a form of avoidance. Feels pressure to succeed and fears not meeting expectations.
